REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND ONLY DUE TO THE ALLEGATION REGARDING PRESCRIPTIVE EASEMENT OVER A CONCRETE WALKWAY. AS STATED IN HANSEN "[S]UCH NONEXCLUSIVE EASEMENTS [ROAD BUILD ACROSS THE PROPERTY OF ANOTHER] DO NOT CREATE THE SAME PROBLEM THAT ARISES WHEN A PURPORTED EASEMENT PREVENTS THE SERVIENT TENEMENT LANDOWNER FROM USING THE LAND FOR ANY "PRACTICAL PURPOSE," DUE TO THE SERVIENT TENEMENT'S ABILITY TO USE THE SAME ROAD FOR DRIVING. HERE, THE CONCRETE WALKWAY COULD BE USED FOR WALKING BY BOTH THE DOMINANT AND SERVIENT TENEMENT. HANSEN V. SANDRIDGE PARTNERS, L.P. (2018) 22 CAL.APP.5TH 1020. IT IS A FACTUAL ISSUE OF WHETHER IT IS SOMETHING THAT MAY HAPPEN AND IS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR A DETERMINATION ON A DEMURRER. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE REST OF THE CLAIMED PRESCRIPTIVE RIGHTS OVER THE ENTIRETY OF THE DEFENDANT'S PROPERTY (E.G. GARDENING, SOCIAL GATHERINGS) OR OVER SPECIFIC PARTS OF THE PROPERTY THAT APPEAR TO BE AVAILABLE FOR USE ONLY TO THE DOMINANT ESTATE (E.G. SHED, FLOWER BED) FALL SQUARELY WITHIN HARRISON DECISION. HARRISON V. WELCH (2004) 116 CAL. APP. 4TH 1084. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EQUITABLE EASEMENT FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION, INCLUDING INNOCENCE OF THE ENCROACHER. PLAINTIFF SHALL BE MINDFUL OF THE SHAM PLEADING DOCTRINE. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT ALLEGE PRESCRIPTIVE EASEMENT AND EQUITABLE EASEMENT IN THE ALTERNATIVE IN THE COMPLAINT AND APPEAR TO CLAIM AN EQUITABLE EASEMENT ARISING OUT OF THE PRESCRIPTIVE EASEMENT WITHOUT CITING ANY AUTHORITY FOR THIS PROPOSITION IN THE OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: CHARLES F, HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alexandra Azad for plaintiff Clarence Vincent Townsend, Jr. (via Zoom).
Johnny Yeh for defendants and cross-complainants Melvin Washington and Masumi Washington (via Zoom).

Mr. Yeh contested the Court's tentative decision on defendants' Demurrer. After hearing oral argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Defendant's authority does not stand for the proposition that for an equitable easement cause of action, an innocent successor-in-interest must allege the innocence of the prior owner who constructed the easement at issue. In each case, the party to the litigation was the party who constructed the easement, and thus was the party whose innocence needed to be alleged. See Dolske v. Gormely (1962) 58 Cal.2d 513, 517; Warsaw v. Chicago Metallics Ceilings, Inc., (1984) 35 Cal.3d 564, 569; Christensen v. Tucker (1952) 114 Cal.App.2d 554, 555; Brown v. Derby Hollywood Corp. v. Hatton (1964) 61 Cal.2d 855, 857.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in accordance with CRC Rule 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Feb-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Johnny Yeh; 415-894-9284; Emergen LLP; appearing as counsel for the defendant.
Jaimie Bombard; 415-984-8200; Nixon Peabody LLP; appearing as counsel for plaintiff.
Reporter: Sheila Pham; CSR# 13293; 415-274-9977; sfdepo@veritext.com

Hearing held (See reporter's transcript for full details).Both parties contest the courts tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MELVIN, WASHINGTON, MASUMI WASHINGTON MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion is DENIED as to the first cause of action and only due to the allegation regarding prescriptive easement over a concrete walkway. As stated in Hansen "[s]uch nonexclusive easements [road build across the property of another] do not create the same problem that arises when a purported easement prevents the servient tenement landowner from using the land for any "practical purpose," due to the servient tenement's ability to use the same road for driving. Here, the concrete walkway could be used for walking by both the dominant and servient tenement. Hansen v. Sandridge Partners, L.P. (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1020. It is a factual issue of whether it is something that may happen and is not appropriate for a determination on a demurrer. The Court notes that the rest of the claimed prescriptive rights over the entirety of the Defendant's property (e.g. gardening, social gatherings) or over specific parts of the property that appear to be available for use only to the dominant estate (e.g. shed, flower bed) fall squarely within Harrison decision. Harrison v. Welch (2004) 116 Cal. App. 4th 1084.
Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend as to the cause of action for equitable easement for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action, including innocence of the encroacher. Plaintiff shall be mindful of the sham pleading doctrine. Plaintiffs did not allege prescriptive easement and equitable easement in the alternative in the Complaint and appear to claim an equitable easement arising out of the prescriptive easement without citing any authority for this proposition in the opposition to the demurrer.
Motion is GRANTED without leave to amend as to the cause of action for declaratory relief as it is wholly derivative of the other causes of action. Plaintiff provided no opposition to this argument raised in the moving papers.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
